items.title: 'How low will they stoop?' Apology demanded over leak to media

items.title: Tasmania to crack down on coercive control with major law reforms

children.content: New laws designed to strengthen family violence protections in Tasmania include an extension to the statutory limitation period for coercive control offences, and a "nation-leading" levy which would be paid by offenders.

image.altText: Silhouette of woman and hands

items.title: Farmers desperate for nitrogen 'drug' search for substitutes

children.content: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

items.title: Hopes inquest into Hillcrest jumping castle tragedy will begin in September

children.content: A coronial inquest into the deaths of six children at a Tasmanian primary school could begin in September, nearly five years after the tragedy occurred.

items.title: Maugean skates released after more than two years in captivity

children.content: A pair of endangered rays taken from a Tasmanian harbour as a species "insurance policy" have been released back into the wild after helping to produce more than 400 eggs for a captive research program.

image.altText: A man holds a Maugean skate in the water

items.title: Preferred bidder announced for troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter

children.content: A consortium headed by Adroit Capital has been announced as the preferred bidder for the smelter in northern Tasmania, as federal and state government pledge another $5 million to support workers in the weeks ahead.

items.title: Meet the Iranians who gather every night outside Khamanei's house

children.content: Outside the Tehran compound that was home to now-dead Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of his supporters gather every night to mourn the only leader many of them have ever known.

items.title: Man joins brother and late mother's reconciliation journey after discovering he is Indigenous

children.content: Iain Seymour didn't realise he was Aboriginal until he discovered he was adopted and learned that his mother played a key role in a memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations.
